beats.activity: Wake up, shower, coffee, check messages
beats.reasoning: Monday morning routine at full energy; Liam wakes early to prepare for the day ahead.

beats.activity: Drive to tattoo studio in Fremont
beats.reasoning: Commute from Capitol Hill to Fremont studio; sunny weather makes for a pleasant drive, and he arrives well before opening.

beats.activity: Open studio, prep equipment, answer client emails, review today's appointments
beats.reasoning: Studio prep and administrative work before first clients arrive; meticulous spreadsheet review of bookings and expenses.

beats.activity: Drive to Capitol Hill bar to catch USA vs Wales World Cup match
beats.reasoning: Light Rail is closed 9am–3pm between Westlake and U-District, so driving is necessary; the 11am FIFA match is a must-watch for Liam, and bars downtown are open early.

beats.activity: Watch USA vs Wales World Cup match, grab lunch and a drink
beats.reasoning: High-energy social beat during the live match; Liam values time with friends and the energy of the crowd during major sporting events, especially on a sunny day.

beats.activity: Drive back to tattoo studio in Fremont
beats.reasoning: Return to studio for afternoon client appointments; Light Rail closure still in effect, so driving required.

beats.activity: Tattoo appointments with clients, consultations, and design work
beats.reasoning: Core work hours with back-to-back client sessions; Liam's patient demeanor and social nature make him excellent at client consultations and detailed design work.
End of workday; drive home in the early evening while weather is still pleasant.

beats.activity: Prepare and eat dinner, update expense spreadsheet
beats.reasoning: Home dinner routine; Liam takes time to meticulously track expenses and relax after a full day of work and social activity.

beats.activity: Open-mic poetry night at local venue, then video gaming at home
beats.reasoning: Evening leisure split: Liam plans to either attend an open-mic poetry event or wind down with video gaming at home; given the full day of social activity and work, a quieter evening at home gaming feels natural.
Wind down and sleep; full 7h 45m rest after a full Monday of work, sports, and socializing.

diary: Woke up early with the sun pouring through the Capitol Hill windows. The day felt restless from the start—couldn't shake the World Cup energy. Made it to the studio by 8:30, prepped the chairs and checked emails, but honestly my head was already at Q Nightclub. Broke protocol and ducked out at ten to catch the USA vs Wales match. The bar was packed with early drinkers, and something about watching the game with strangers while nursing a cold beer felt necessary, you know? Grabbed a burger too. Got back to the studio a little buzzed and surprisingly focused—did three solid pieces that afternoon, including a killer geometric sleeve. By evening I was fried but didn't want the day to end, so I hit the open-mic poetry thing downtown. Some kid read this piece about gentrification that hit different. Came home, lost two hours to some new game, then crashed hard. Good day, weird rhythm.
thought_process: I almost felt guilty leaving the studio mid-morning, but the World Cup match was calling—it's not every day USA plays early, and I knew the bars would be buzzing. I needed that social break, that collective energy. Work has been steady, and I don't regret the detour; it actually reset me. The afternoon appointments went smooth, like the break had recharged something. By evening I was chasing that stimulation again—poetry night, gaming—trying to hold onto the momentum of a sunny Monday. The Light Rail closure didn't affect me since I drive, but it made the city feel smaller somehow, more contained. Mood-wise, I rode a wave of restlessness and satisfaction, never quite settling but never frustrated either.
world_event_prompt: Sunny Monday in early June, high of 74F. FIFA World Cup group stage match (USA vs Wales) airs at 11am PDT — bars downtown open early. Light Rail closed between Westlake and University District 9am-3pm for maintenance.
